title: Ingest Callan-Harvey And Jackiw-Rebbi For The Edge-Mode Proof
summary: Return to L0 only for denser equation-level archival mapping of Callan-Harvey and Jackiw-Rebbi and for the broader continuum-to-lattice bridge; the core Lecture Three continuum theorem family is source-grade locally and now includes an explicit half-level theorem, hemisphere-Berry-flux fragment, Callan-Harvey cancellation fragment, and lecture-one boundary precursor.

candidate_sources.citation: C. G. Callan Jr. and J. A. Harvey, "Anomalies And Fermion Zero-Modes On Strings And Domain Walls," Nucl. Phys. B250 (1985) 427.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites anomaly inflow as the boundary-consistency mechanism.

candidate_sources.citation: R. Jackiw and C. Rebbi, "Solitons With Fermion Number 1/2," Phys. Rev. D13 (1976) 3398.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites Jackiw-Rebbi for the sign-changing mass domain-wall mode.

title: Ingest FQHE Fractional Charge And Statistics
summary: Return to L0 and ingest focused fractional-Hall sources so the deferred fractional-charge and anyonic-statistics branch can later leave the future buffer.

candidate_sources.citation: X.-G. Wen, "Topological Orders and Edge Excitations in FQH States," Adv. Phys. 44 (1995) 405.
candidate_sources.why: Wen's review is the designated recovery source for fractional charge, statistics, and related topological-order data.

title: Ingest FQHE Topological Order And Wilson-Loop Algebra
summary: Return to L0 and ingest a broad fractional-Hall background source so the split Wilson-loop, torus-degeneracy, and fractional-charge branches can later leave the future buffer with more focused follow-up tasks.

candidate_sources.citation: X.-G. Wen, "Topological Orders and Edge Excitations in FQH States," Adv. Phys. 44 (1995) 405.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites Wen as the review-level source for the effective-field-theory and topological-order structure of the fractional Hall effect.

title: Ingest FQHE Wilson-Loop Algebra And Torus Degeneracy
summary: Return to L0 and ingest focused fractional-Hall sources so the deferred Wilson-loop algebra and torus-degeneracy branches can later leave the future buffer.

candidate_sources.citation: X.-G. Wen, "Topological Orders and Edge Excitations in FQH States," Adv. Phys. 44 (1995) 405.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites Wen as the review-level source for effective gauge theory, Wilson loops, and topological degeneracy.

title: Ingest Friedan's Proof Of Nielsen-Ninomiya
summary: This cited-source recovery task has now been resolved into local L2-grade units: Friedan's spectral-projection current and Chern-character / cobordism route are explicit inside the Lecture One theorem family.

candidate_sources.citation: D. Friedan, "A Proof Of The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem," Commun. Math. Phys. 85 (1982) 481.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites Friedan as a stronger proof route for the theorem.

title: Ingest TKNN And Haldane For The Lecture Two Equivalence
summary: Return to L0 only for denser equation-level archival mapping of TKNN and the original Haldane paper; the core Lecture Two theorem family is source-grade locally through the traced-curvature identity, the occupied-projector formula, and the many-body route, and the Haldane bridge is already decomposed through graphene Hamiltonian, Dirac-point, symmetry-protection, and valley-mass Chern-number units.

candidate_sources.citation: D. J. Thouless, M. Kohmoto, M. P. Nightingale, and M. den Nijs, "Quantized Hall Conductance In A Two-Dimensional Periodic Potential," Phys. Rev. Lett. 49 (1982) 405.
candidate_sources.why: Primary source for the band-theory Hall-conductance invariant.

candidate_sources.citation: F. D. M. Haldane, "Model For A Quantum Hall Effect Without Landau Levels: Condensed Matter Realization Of The Parity Anomaly," Phys. Rev. Lett. 61 (1988) 2016.
candidate_sources.why: Witten explicitly points to Haldane for a later conceptual route to the same relation and to boundary spectral flow.
